title: Choosing Between SQL and NoSQL Databases

questions.sentence: Although front end developers primarily work with the UI understanding how data is stored in the back end is vital for building efficient applications
Databases are generally divided into two main types SQL and NoSQL
SQL databases like PostgreSQL or MySQL are relational meaning they store data in tables with predefined schemas
On the other hand NoSQL databases like MongoDB or Firebase Firestore are non relational and store data in a more flexible format often resembling JSON objects
Choosing the right database depends on the specific needs of your project such as data complexity scalability requirements and the speed of development
